nodes.name: Exhibit Authoring
nodes.summary: Browser-based Studio for creating libraries and exhibits: import media (images, maps, audio, video), arrange objects on a canvas, draw spatial and temporal annotation regions, write notes, organize narrative sections, and manage competing interpretive Readings.

businessRules: One exhibit holds many objects (images, maps, audio, video)
Readings are mutually exclusive interpretive passes over an object
Playground vs Project is per-exhibit (example=template/playground, user-created=project)
Notes target library, exhibit, object, region, time-range, or geographic region
Map regions use longitude/latitude as source of truth (geo-truth)
crossDomainInteractions: Feeds annotations into the Annotation Spine for persistence
Reads from IIIF Publishing to project exhibits for preview
Uses Rendering Adapters for canvas display in the editor viewport

nodes.name: Annotation Spine
nodes.summary: Append-only annotation log with version-parent DAG enabling non-destructive edits, concurrent merge, and full version history. Every create, edit, and delete produces a new append entry; annotations are versioned and linkable with cite-from-cite support.

businessRules: All edits are non-destructive — they produce new append entries
Version-parent DAG enables concurrent merge without data loss
W3C Web Annotation Data Model (WADM) is the canonical format
Session wraps the log in transactional CRUD with conflict detection
@context appears ONLY at AnnotationPage level, never on individual Annotations
crossDomainInteractions: Receives create/edit/delete operations from Exhibit Authoring
Fed into IIIF Publishing to produce IIIF AnnotationPages
Read by Viewer Presentation to display annotations on the canvas

nodes.name: IIIF Publishing
nodes.summary: Projects exhibits to IIIF Presentation 3 manifests and collections, builds the static site tree, and exports portable .archie.zip archives. Bridges Archie's internal model to the interoperable IIIF standard for third-party tool compatibility.

businessRules: Exhibits become IIIF Presentation 3 manifests
Libraries become IIIF Collections
Published artifact is self-describing: static per-exhibit HTML with per-note anchors
Portable .archie.zip contains the entire published artifact
Published site has no backend — static HTML + JSON + media files
crossDomainInteractions: Consumes exhibit structure from Exhibit Authoring
Output consumed by Viewer Presentation for display

nodes.name: Viewer Presentation
nodes.summary: Astro-built static shell that renders any published library at runtime — gallery browse, deep-zoom reading with annotation overlays, hash-based deep-linking, narrative reading mode, and portable zip mode. Supports hosted (GitHub Pages) and offline (zip) deployments.

businessRules: Viewer reads the working store live — one canonical store, two same-origin apps
Publish = durability only; Viewer projects working store through the same pipeline
One shell handles both hosted and portable (zip) modes
Hash-based routing enables deep-linking to specific exhibits, objects, and notes
Readings are presented as a radio legend — reader picks one, canvas re-frames
crossDomainInteractions: Reads published exhibits from IIIF Publishing output
Uses Rendering Adapters for OpenSeadragon/Annotorious canvas
Shares same-origin store with Exhibit Authoring (Studio) in dev mode

nodes.name: Media Processing
nodes.summary: Handles EXIF orientation normalization (bakes upright display masters on import) and AV transcript import (WebVTT/SRT → timed annotation notes). Classifies image sources and resolves tile-source descriptors for IIIF Image API services.

businessRules: EXIF orientation is normalized on import to produce upright display masters
WebVTT/SRT transcripts are imported as timed annotation notes
Image sources classified as direct URLs vs IIIF Image API services
Audio and video objects play inline with a transcript spine
Combined spatiotemporal selector for video annotation (box + time window)
crossDomainInteractions: Used by Exhibit Authoring during media import
Used by IIIF Publishing for tile-source resolution
Used by Viewer Presentation for media playback

nodes.name: Create Exhibit
nodes.summary: Author creates a new exhibit: selects media, arranges objects on the overview canvas, configures the layout type, and sets exhibit metadata.

nodes.name: Annotate Object
nodes.summary: Author opens an object in the editor, draws spatial (rectangle/polygon) or temporal (time-range) regions, writes notes in the popover, and manages Readings.

nodes.name: Build Narrative
nodes.summary: Author organizes sections into a narrative spine that walks readers from object to object, with prose interspersed between annotation views.

nodes.name: Append Annotation
nodes.summary: Annotation log receives create/edit/delete operations, produces properly sequenced entries with version-parent references, and resolves concurrent edits via DAG merge.

nodes.name: Project to IIIF
nodes.summary: Exhibit and its annotations are projected to IIIF Presentation 3: Canvases for each object, painting Annotations, tile-source descriptors, and rights statements.

nodes.name: Build Static Site
nodes.summary: Publishing pipeline serializes the IIIF Collection and Manifests to disk, writes per-exhibit annotation pages, and assembles the complete directory structure for deployment.

domainMeta.entryPoint: publish/site.ts — serialize IIIF tree to disk

nodes.name: Browse Gallery
nodes.summary: Visitor lands on the library gallery, browses published exhibits with cover thumbnails, and selects an exhibit to view.

nodes.name: Read Exhibit
nodes.summary: Visitor views an exhibit: deep-zoom canvas with annotation overlays, Readings legend for switching interpretations, narrative spine for prose-led reading, media playback for audio/video.

nodes.name: Import Media
nodes.summary: Media files are imported: EXIF orientation is normalized for images, transcripts are parsed from WebVTT/SRT into timed annotation notes, and image sources are classified for IIIF resolution.

nodes.name: Initialize Library
nodes.summary: LibraryHome.svelte presents the library shell; store.ts initializes the OPFS working store for exhibit metadata persistence.

nodes.name: Import Media
nodes.summary: MediaPicker.svelte imports image folders, IIIF manifest URLs, or pastes external IIIF sources. EXIF normalization runs on import.

nodes.name: Arrange Objects
nodes.summary: ExhibitOverview.svelte renders the zoomable overview canvas where objects are placed by drag; layout type is auto-detected.

nodes.name: Set Metadata
nodes.summary: DetailsEditor.svelte configures exhibit title, description, rights, and attribution metadata.

nodes.name: Open Object Editor
nodes.summary: App.svelte routes to the object editor; Canvas.svelte renders the deep-zoom surface with Annotorious overlay.

nodes.name: Draw Region
nodes.summary: Author draws a rectangle, polygon, or time-range selector on the canvas; DrawTool and SelectionId from render-mount provide the tool primitives.

nodes.name: Write Note
nodes.summary: NoteEditor.svelte popover appears anchored to the marker; author writes body, sets motivation (describing, commenting, etc.), and saves.

nodes.name: Append to Spine
nodes.summary: Spine session.ts wraps the log append; annotation enters the version DAG with conflict detection.

nodes.name: Manage Readings
nodes.summary: ReadingsEditor.svelte assigns annotations to mutually exclusive Readings; ReadingsRail.svelte shows the toggleable legend.

nodes.name: Create Sections
nodes.summary: NarrativeEditor.svelte creates Section objects; each Section is a self-contained third layer (not a tour of Notes).

nodes.name: Arrange Order
nodes.summary: Sections are ordered in the narrative spine; the ordering drives the Viewer navigation flow.

nodes.name: Serialize as Annotation
nodes.summary: Each Section serializes as a supplementing WADM annotation (additive; Range stays canonical) — ships twice: IIIF Range + derived annotation.

nodes.name: Append Operation
nodes.summary: log.ts appendNew/appendEdit/appendDelete produce new log entries; each references its version parent in the DAG.

nodes.name: Conflict Detection
nodes.summary: session.ts wraps the log in transactional CRUD; detects concurrent edit conflicts and merges safely.

nodes.name: Serialize Annotation
nodes.summary: serialize.ts converts internal annotation records to W3C WADM JSON, handling body/target/motivation serialization.

nodes.name: Build Canvases
nodes.summary: manifest.ts maps each Exhibit Object to a IIIF Canvas with dimensions, media type, and painting annotations.

nodes.name: Resolve Tile Sources
nodes.summary: resolve.ts classifies image sources (direct URL vs IIIF Image API) and generates tile-source descriptors with XYZ template URLs.

nodes.name: Project Annotations
nodes.summary: Annotations are projected as IIIF AnnotationPages; each Reading becomes an AnnotationPage grouped by AnnotationCollection.

nodes.name: Attach Rights
nodes.summary: rights.ts attaches IIIF rights and requiredStatement metadata with attribution and license details.

nodes.name: Serialize Site Tree
nodes.summary: site.ts writes IIIF Collection and Manifests to disk, builds the per-exhibit annotation pages directory, assembles the complete deployable tree.

nodes.name: Generate HTML Entry Points
nodes.summary: gen-published.mts generates per-exhibit HTML entry points with embedded navigation and annotation displays.

nodes.name: Package Portable Zip
nodes.summary: ghpages.ts / portable.ts create the self-contained .archie.zip with all manifests, annotations, media references, and HTML.

nodes.name: Load Library Index
nodes.summary: ViewerShell detects deployment mode (hosted vs portable), loads the library index, and routes to the gallery view.

nodes.name: Render Exhibit Cards
nodes.summary: Gallery.svelte renders published exhibits as cards with thumbnails, titles, descriptions, and Reading labels.

nodes.name: Select Exhibit
nodes.summary: Hash-based route.ts parses the selection into a structured ViewerRoute, deep-linking to the specific exhibit.

nodes.name: Load Exhibit
nodes.summary: ExhibitView.svelte loads published exhibit data, resolves layout type (single, grid, narrative), and initializes the appropriate view.

nodes.name: Render Canvas
nodes.summary: Canvas.svelte instantiates OpenSeadragon viewer via createCanvasController, overlays Annotorious annotation layer, and renders regions.

nodes.name: Show Annotations
nodes.summary: NoteLightbox.svelte displays annotation bodies in a modal; NoteMedia.svelte renders attached media; Credit.svelte shows IIIF attribution.

nodes.name: Switch Reading
nodes.summary: Visitor toggles the Readings legend (radio); canvas re-frames to show only the selected Reading's annotations.

nodes.name: Follow Narrative
nodes.summary: NarrativeReader.svelte renders the prose spine; selecting a beat drives the canvas to that region.

nodes.name: Play Media
nodes.summary: MediaPlayer.svelte plays audio/video objects inline with transcript spine; each transcript line seeks the media to its time.

nodes.name: Normalize EXIF
nodes.summary: Image import reads EXIF orientation and bakes an upright display master so the Viewer always renders correctly.

nodes.name: Parse Transcript
nodes.summary: WebVTT/SRT files are parsed into timed annotation notes; mediafragment.ts handles the fragment selectors for time ranges.

nodes.name: Classify Image Source
nodes.summary: resolve.ts determines if an image is a direct URL or IIIF Image API service; generates appropriate tile-source descriptors.

edges.description: Studio writes annotations through the spine for persistence and versioning
Studio triggers media import, EXIF normalization, and transcript parsing
Annotated exhibits are projected to IIIF manifests for publication
Published IIIF manifests and static site are consumed by the Viewer
Resolved tile sources and normalized media feed into IIIF manifest generation
Studio working store is read live by Viewer on same origin (dev loop)
